name: Netty Removal from mercury-services-commons
description: Complete removal of Netty (io.netty:* and netty-nio-client) from cloud-sdk-aws module, replacing with AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient. Eliminates 9 HIGH OWASP CVEs. Version bumped from 1.0.22-SNAPSHOT to 1.0.23-SNAPSHOT.

entries.summary: Investigated Netty usage across all modules — only cloud-sdk-aws had Netty references
entries.detail:
Investigated all three modules for Netty usage:
- cloud-sdk-api: No Netty references in POM, source, or tests. No changes needed.
- commons: No Netty references in POM, source, or tests. Confirmed clean.
- cloud-sdk-aws: Netty found in 1 production file (AwsHttpClientWrapper.java), 3 test files (TransferManagerFactoryTest, S3ClientFactoryTest, AwsStorageConfigTest), and POM (10 dependencies: netty-nio-client + 9 io.netty artifacts at 4.1.124.Final).
- AwsHttpClientWrapperTest already used AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient — no changes needed.

entries.summary: Removed all Netty dependencies and replaced with AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient in production and test code
entries.detail:
POM changes:
- Removed 10 dependencies from cloud-sdk-aws/pom.xml: netty-nio-client, netty-codec-http2, netty-handler, netty-common, netty-buffer, netty-transport, netty-codec, netty-codec-http, netty-resolver, netty-transport-native-unix-common (all 4.1.124.Final)
- aws-crt-client was already declared — no additions needed

Production code (AwsHttpClientWrapper.java):
- Replaced import: NettyNioAsyncHttpClient → AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient
- Removed DEFAULT_TLS_NEGOTIATION_TIMEOUT_MILLIS import (CRT handles TLS internally)
- Updated defaultAsyncClient(): NettyNioAsyncHttpClient.builder() → AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient.builder(), removed .tlsNegotiationTimeout() call

Test code changes:
- TransferManagerFactoryTest.java: Replaced NettyNioAsyncHttpClient with AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient (import, builder, 2 assertions)
- S3ClientFactoryTest.java: Removed Netty import, updated 1 assertion
- AwsStorageConfigTest.java: Removed Netty import, removed createNettyHttpClient() helper, updated test data to use existing createCrtAsyncHttpClient()

Version bump in root pom.xml: 1.0.22-SNAPSHOT → 1.0.23-SNAPSHOT

entries.summary: All tests passed, verify succeeded for all modules, installed to local Maven repo
entries.detail:

Command 2: mvn clean verify (all modules)

Result: BUILD SUCCESS — All 10 modules installed to local .m2 repository (15.542s)

entries.summary: Decision: Remove Netty entirely rather than upgrading — eliminates 5 HIGH CVEs across 9 artifacts
entries.detail:
Chose complete Netty removal (Section 10 of OWASP doc) over Netty upgrade (Section 4, Fix 1) because:
1. Eliminates all 9 Netty CVEs permanently — no future Netty CVE treadmill
2. aws-crt-client was already a dependency and already used in tests
3. AWS recommends CRT over Netty for S3 transfers
4. Smaller dependency footprint (removes 10 JARs from classpath)
5. Lower maintenance burden — no more explicit Netty version pins to track

CVEs eliminated: CVE-2026-33871, CVE-2026-33870, CVE-2025-58057, CVE-2025-67735, CVE-2025-58056

Documentation created: cloud-sdk-aws/docs/2026-04-23-netty-removal.md

entries.summary: Transitive Netty still present via AWS SDK BOM — fixed with exclusions on all 13 AWS SDK dependencies in cloud-sdk-aws
entries.detail:
After integrating cloud-sdk-aws:1.0.23-SNAPSHOT into the booking module, Netty was still present at runtime. Root cause: removing the direct netty-nio-client dependency only removed the explicit declaration — all AWS SDK v2 service clients (ssm, sesv2, s3, sns, sqs, dynamodb, sso, ssooidc, etc.) still pull netty-nio-client transitively through the AWS SDK BOM (2.30.24). The Netty version also regressed from pinned 4.1.124.Final to BOM default 4.1.118.Final.

Fix: Added exclusion for software.amazon.awssdk:netty-nio-client to all 13 AWS SDK v2 dependencies in cloud-sdk-aws/pom.xml (ssm, sesv2, s3, s3-transfer-manager, sns, sqs, dynamodb, dynamodb-enhanced, apache-client, url-connection-client, aws-crt-client, sso, ssooidc). This ensures Netty is excluded at the library level so no downstream consumer needs exclusions.

No functionality impact — all sync services use ApacheHttpClient, async uses AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient (already migrated), and AWS SDK SPI will discover CRT as the async provider.
